text.content:
DM tool. The faint orange scrawls left by other Unkindled across worlds. Drop these as scene notes / journal pins for atmosphere and the occasional hint — some true, some useless, a few outright lies, exactly as in the games. Reveal one or two as the party enters a space; never explain them. They make the world feel haunted and lived-in.

Genuinely helpful

  • "Try tail, then weakness." (near the Dragon)
  • "Parry, then visions of triumph." (Gundyr / Sword Master)
  • "Fire ahead." (before a Pus of Man)
  • "Be wary of charge." (Outrider / Vordt)
  • "Don't give up, skeleton!" (after a wipe)
  • "Time for crab... no, cold." (approaching Vordt)

Atmosphere / lore

  • "Ahh, grave ahead."
  • "Therefore, hollow."
  • "Praise the Sun!"
  • "Could this be a bonfire?"
  • "Likely a Lord of Cinder..."
  • "Visions of home."

Lies & troll messages (use sparingly)

  • "Amazing chest ahead." (there is no chest)
  • "Try jumping." (off the rampart)
  • "Hidden path ahead." (a wall)
  • "Treasure straight ahead." (a cliff)
  • "Try hole."

Players can be invited to leave their own message when they rest at a bonfire — a nice bit of player-authored flavor for the table.
